zh.title: Solana/美元技术分析：震荡偏弱整理
zh.content:
当前SOL价格围绕80.4震荡整理，整体处于均线下方，短线趋势偏弱，市场动能不足，呈横盘偏空格局。需关注后续方向选择波动收敛，

MACD为负值且动能柱走弱，显示空头压力仍在延续；价格持续低于SMA20（81.44），中期均线压制明显，反弹缺乏量能配合。

下方关键支撑位于80.20与80.00整数关口，一旦跌破或将测试79.50区域；上方阻力集中在81.4均线及82.0附近，突破需放量确认。

操作上建议以区间交易为主，未有效站上81.4前保持反弹做空思路，若跌破80.20则顺势加仓空单，严格控制风险并关注止损执行纪律。

en.title: Solana vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
Solana vs USD is currently exhibiting a narrow consolidation around 80.43 after a prolonged cooling phase. Price action remains tightly compressed between 80.27 and 80.45, reflecting subdued volatility and indecision. Trading below the 20-day SMA at 81.44 reinforces a mild bearish bias in the short term. Overall structure suggests the market is attempting to stabilize after recent downside pressure while lacking strong bullish momentum to reclaim higher levels.

Momentum indicators reinforce this cautious outlook, with MACD reading -0.385 signaling persistent bearish pressure. The absence of a visible signal line value limits crossover confirmation, but the negative histogram context suggests downside momentum is still dominant. With RSI unavailable, price behavior near the lower end of its tight range implies neutral-to-weak momentum rather than strong reversal conditions. Buyers have not yet demonstrated sufficient strength to shift trend structure upward.

Key support and resistance levels define a tight trading corridor for SOL/USD. Immediate support is located at 80.27, with psychological reinforcement near the 80.00 handle and deeper risk around 79.50 if breakdown pressure increases. On the upside, resistance is first encountered at the 20-day SMA of 81.44, followed by 82.00 and 83.00 as subsequent supply zones where sellers may re-emerge.

Given the current structure, SOL/USD is best approached as a range-bound market with a slight bearish tilt. Short-term traders may consider buying near support around 80.00 with tight risk controls, while fading rallies toward 81.40–82.00 remains viable. A breakout above 82.00 would be required to invalidate the bearish bias, whereas a sustained break below 79.50 could accelerate downside momentum further.
